author: Ro_Patel
post:
Rumour: Trump Admin plans 30-day waiver of the Jones Act 
 
The Merchant Marine Act of 1920 (Jones Act) requires that all goods transported b/n US ports be carried on ships that are: 
 
1) US-built 
2) US-flagged 
3) US-owned (at least 75%) 
4) US-crewed 
 
Historical precedent for this exists—most notably after Hurricane Harvey (2017) & the Colonial Pipeline cyberattack (2021)—but a 30-day wartime waiver is rare & underscores the severity of the current geopolitical climate

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$PSX Phillips 66 appoints Ungerleider, Meyers to Board of Directors 
 
Phillips 66 announced that it has appointed Howard Ungerleider and Kevin Meyers to serve on the Company's Board of Directors, effective immediately. As previously announced, Glenn Tilton and Marna Whittington, Class II directors with terms concluding in 2026, have informed the Company that they intend to retire and will not stand for re-election to the Phillips 66 Board of Directors at the Company's next annual meeting of shareholders in May 2026. Following the annual meeting, the Board will consist of 14 directors, 13 of whom are independent. Ungerleider will serve as a Class II director and Meyers will serve as a Class III director, and the Company's director nominees for election at its 2026 annual meeting of shareholders will be Ungerleider and the Company's other Class II directors, Gregory J. Hayes, Charles M. Holley and Denise R. Singleton.

author: topstockalerts
post:
Jet fuel prices are surging as supply fears ripple through energy markets after the U.S. strikes on Iran, boosting refining margins and driving gains in U.S. refinery stocks. Benchmark jet fuel in Singapore has jumped to about $147 per barrel from around $90 earlier this winter, while New York Harbor prices have climbed to roughly $150 per barrel, according to market data. 
Shipping congestion near the Strait of Hormuz—which handles about 20% of global oil consumption—and refinery disruptions in the Middle East are tightening supply. Some Asian refiners are cutting production due to uncertain crude deliveries, while refinery disruptions in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait are adding to the squeeze. 
Jet fuel markets typically react first to supply shocks because inventories are relatively small. At the same time, China and India are reportedly reducing fuel exports, further tightening regional supply. 
$PBF  $MPC $VLO  $PSX  $PARR

author: topstockalerts
post:
Venezuela’s regime change has renewed debate over how a gradual normalization of its oil industry under U.S. influence could affect energy markets. Although sanctions remain in place, U.S. involvement in rebuilding Venezuela’s oil infrastructure could eventually open access to heavy crude. In the near term, oil prices are unlikely to move, as OPEC+ policy, Russian exports, and global demand remain the primary drivers. 
The most immediate beneficiaries would be U.S. Gulf Coast refiners, which are configured to process Venezuela’s heavy, sulfur-rich crude. Even limited, reliable supply could improve feedstock flexibility and margins. Over the longer term, Canadian heavy crude producers could face renewed competition, potentially limiting price differentials and pressuring margins, while U.S. shale producers remain largely unaffected due to their focus on light crude. 
$VLO $PBF  $CVX  $PSX  $SU

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$LLY $BABA $PSX $MSFT $RIO  
 
TOP 5 WEEKEND STORIES: 
 
1) On Truth Social, President Donald Trump wrote, "Therefore, I am pleased to announce that Tomorrow morning, in the White House, at 9:00 A.M., I will be signing one of the most consequential Executive Orders in our Country's history. Prescription Drug and Pharmaceutical prices will be REDUCED, almost immediately, by 30% to 80%." Publicly traded companies in the pharma space include AstraZeneca (AZN), Bristol Myers (BMY), Eli Lilly (LLY), GSK (GSK), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Novartis (NVS), Pfizer (PFE), Roche (RHHBY) and Sanofi (SNY).  
 
2) U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessen reported "substantial progress" in U.S. talks with China's officials to de-escalate a trade war, but offered no details of an agreement reached, Emma Farge and John Revill of Reuters reports. Bessen told reporters further details would be announced on Monday and that President Donald Trump was aware of the results of the "productive talks." Chinese companies that publicly trade in the U.S. include Alibaba (BABA), Yum China (YUMC), Tencent (TCEHY), NetEase (NTES), PDD (PDD), Nio (NIO), Baidu (BIDU), and JD.com (JD).  
 
3) Elliott Investment Management, which manages funds that together make it a top-five shareholder in Phillips 66 (PSX), announced that leading independent proxy advisory firm Glass, Lewis & Co. has recommended shareholders support meaningful boardroom change by voting for the election of three Elliott director nominees - Brian Coffman, Sigmund Cornelius and Michael Heim - to the Company's Board of Directors at the 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholder.  
 
4) OpenAI and Microsoft (MSFT) are rewriting the terms of their partnership in a negotiation made to allow OpenAI to launch a future initial public offering while protecting Microsoft's access to its AI models, George Hammond, Stephen Morris, Cristina Criddle, and Melissa Heikkila of The Financial Times reports. Microsoft is offering to give up some of its equity stake in OpenAI's new for-profit business in exchange for accessing new technology beyond the original deal's 2030 cut off, three people with direct knowledge of the discussions said.  
 
5) A U.S. federal judge temporarily blocked the Trump administration from transferring land to Rio Tinto (RIO) and BHP (BHP) for a copper mine opposed by Native Americans, Ernest Scheyder of Reuters reports. The judge cited the Supreme Court's ongoing deliberations in the case as reason for the block.

author: ChessGM
post:
Heads up alert! Upcoming earnings on Friday, 4/25/2025 for $PSX Bearish (2.7) 
 
Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX) has faced significant challenges recently, with its share price declining by approximately 23% over the past month, and currently trading around $126 after a brief recovery. 
The company’s fundamentals, while showing some resilience, are under scrutiny as it prepares to report its first-quarter earnings. 
Analysts anticipate a decline in earnings due to a combination of market volatility and operational pressures, particularly regarding the company's recent $2.2 billion acquisition of EPIC Y-Grade GP, which is expected to enhance its operational capabilities but comes with integration risks. 
The company's P/E ratio currently hovers around 10, which is competitive within its industry but raises questions regarding future growth prospects, especially given the expected decline in EPS. 
Furthermore, Phillips 66's revenue forecasts have been tempered by pressures from activist investor Elliott Management, which has advocated for board changes and operational improvements, signaling potential governance issues that may affect long-term performance. 
Comparatively, peers such as Valero and Marathon Oil have shown more stable performance metrics, raising concerns about Phillips 66’s relative positioning within the energy sector. 
In terms of upcoming earnings reports, Phillips 66 is scheduled to announce its financial results on April 25, 2025. 
Expectations are muted, with analysts predicting a potential drop in earnings as the company navigates through challenges stemming from its recent strategic moves and broader market conditions. 
Historically, Phillips 66 has shown resilience, but recent trends indicate a shift in investor sentiment, particularly influenced by Elliott's aggressive push for change. 
The consensus among analysts suggests a cautious outlook, with many urging investors to assess the risks associated with the company's governance and operational strategies as they prepare for the earnings announcement. 
The potential impact on stock performance could be significant, particularly if the earnings report fails to meet the low expectations set by analysts.

Phillips 66 is expected to nominate two new directors to its board amid an intensifying proxy fight with activist Elliott Investment Management, The Wall Street Journal's Lauren Thomas reports. Phillips 66 is set to disclose four nominees, including the two new ones, in its proxy materials Wednesday morning, according to people familiar with the matter. author: Valuation_Finance
post:
$PSX $MPC $CVX For the week, refiners are mostly down 1.5 to 3.5% when WTI and Brent fell ~1.8%.  However, Phillips 66 rose 1.6%. PSX is being pressured by Elliott Investment to sell its stake in Chevron Phillips Chemical Co., which is an equal joint venture. Elliott is seeking to raise $15 billion from the sale of the petrochemical unit. The logical buyer would be Chevron, who has the right of first refusal.  There are reports that Chevron wants to buy out the stake.  
 
If so, the sale would allow PSX to focus on the refining business and reduce its "inefficient conglomerate structure," which Elliott claims is responsible for poor stock price performance. No word whether PSX's management is open to the divestiture.

Phillips 66 accussed of violating Clean Water Act, NY Times reports 
 
A federal grand jury indicted Phillips 66 on charges of violating the Clean Water Act by dumping almost 800,000 gallons of contaminated waste into the Los Angeles County sewer system and failing to report the incident, Cade Metz of The New York Times reports. The company was charged with having released wastewater with excessive amounts of oil and grease from a refinery in California in 2020 and 2021, the Times said, citing the Justic Department. The company faces up to five years of probation for each of 6 counts and a maximum of $2.4M in mines.

author: Swing500Trading
post:
$PSX $SPY $VIX 
 
As we know, the election is heavily hedged, with participants favoring index puts over the past two weeks, which has led to sell flows from a dealer perspective.  
 
However, what happens when these put hedges are unwound or start to lose value as the market moves higher and implied volatility (IV) contracts?  
 
In such a scenario, dealers will be forced to buy back their hedges—either partially or fully—which will trigger buy flows, potentially amplifying upward momentum.

author: ChessGM
post:
$PSX Upcoming Earnings Alert for 10/29/2024 
 
Neutral (5.3) 
Phillips 66 has been navigating a challenging landscape, as indicated by a series of recent developments impacting its stock performance and market perception. 
The company's share price has seen fluctuations, with a notable decline despite broader market gains, suggesting investor uncertainty. 
Analysts are concerned about the upcoming Q3 earnings report, as forecasts predict a significant drop in earnings per share, from $4.63 in the previous year to an anticipated $2.10. 
Additionally, the company faces operational challenges, including the decision to close its Los Angeles refinery due to low profits and market dynamics. 
This closure, alongside a $604.9 million jury verdict for misappropriating trade secrets, has raised further concerns about Phillips 66's operational integrity and financial health. 
While there have been initiatives aimed at divesting non-core assets to enhance returns, the overall sentiment remains cautious, reflecting mixed investor confidence. 
In terms of upcoming earnings, Phillips 66 is set to announce its Q3 financial results on October 29, 2024. 
Analysts expect the company to report earnings of $2.10 per share, a considerable decrease from the $4.63 per share recorded in the same quarter of the previous year. 
Revenue projections are also down, estimated at $34.75 billion compared to $40.32 billion last year. 
This anticipated decline underscores concerns regarding the company's profitability amidst ongoing market pressures, including lower crude prices and high turnaround costs. 
The earnings report will be pivotal for stakeholders, as it will provide insights into Phillips 66's ability to navigate these challenges effectively.

$XOM $PSX $OXY Oil companies urge Trump not to slash Biden's IRA, WSJ reports 
 
Some oil companies are trying to persuade former President Donald Trump and his Republican allies not to slash provisions of the Inflation Reduction Act potentially worth billions to the oil industry, The Wall Street Journal's Collin Eaton and Benoit Morenne report. In talks with Trump's campaign and his allies in Congress, Exxon Mobil (XOM), Phillips 66 (PSX) and Occidental Petroleum (OXY) have praised the benefits of the IRA, with Occidental CEO Vicki Hollub saying tax credits propping up the company's huge investments in technology to collect carbon directly from the air should be preserved, people familiar with the matter said.

BIG OIL URGES TRUMP TO PRESERVE KEY PARTS OF BIDEN'S IRA 
 
Exxon $XOM, Phillips 66 $PSX, and Occidental $OXY are pushing Trump and Republicans to keep parts of Biden's Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) that benefit the oil industry, like tax credits for carbon capture and renewable fuels.  
 
Occidental's CEO Vicki Hollub even lobbied Trump directly to save the subsidies that support its $1.3B direct-air capture project.

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$PSX Phillips 66 reinstated with a Peer Perform at Wolfe Research  
 
Wolfe Research reinstated coverage of Phillips 66 with a Peer Perform rating as part of a broader research note. The firm is "leaning defensive" as oil price is expected to remain rangebound around $85, exacerbated by paper markets testing OPEC+ policy, though the opposite is true for U.S. gas where a contango curve is signaling a structural shift that can reverse a decade of declines in the marginal cost of supply, the analyst tells investors in a research note. Wolfe adds that it sees E&Ps with collectively the best risk / reward in the sector - eventually.

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$PSX Phillips 66 price target lowered to $145 from $156 at Scotiabank 
 
Scotiabank lowered the firm's price target on Phillips 66 to $145 from $156 and keeps an Outperform rating on the shares. The analyst believes consensus estimates are too high for the integrated oil, refining and exploration and production space heading into the Q2 reports. Estimates will need to revise lower over the next couple of weeks, the analyst tells investors in a research note. The firm cites "surprisingly weak" refining and lower gas realizations for the target cuts.

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$PSX Phillips 66 to sell 25% interest in REX to Tallgrass Energy for $1.275B 
 
Phillips 66 announced it has agreed to sell its 25% non-operated common equity interest in Rockies Express Pipeline, or REX, to a subsidiary of Tallgrass Energy for an enterprise value of approximately $1.275B. TGE is the operator of REX and will own 100% of the common equity interest in REX following the transaction. This transaction generates pre-tax cash proceeds of $685M to Phillips 66 after adjustments for Phillips 66's allocation of REX's debt and preferred equity balances.  
 
The expected proceeds will support Phillips 66's strategic priorities, including returns to shareholders. REX, a 1,714-mile pipeline system, is one of the largest natural gas pipelines in the United States and provides over 5 billion cubic feet per day of bi-directional natural gas transportation service between the Rockies, Appalachia and the northeastern United States. The transaction is expected to close today.

author: davooo
post:
$PSX Now focus is on the OPEC+ meeting today, where members are expected to stay the course of limited oil production until the end of June, at least. This means persistently tight supply that will likely push prices higher still, especially as neither Ukraine has any intention to stop droning Russian refineries nor Israel has any intention of changing course in what increasingly seems like a war on all anti-Israel groups in the Middle East.

Even so, some analysts still expect lower prices—because of Chinese demand. “Brent oil futures should track closer to $75 to $80 a barrel in coming months given our view that China’s oil demand growth will disappoint,” Commonwealth Bank of Australia analyst Vivek Dhar told Bloomberg earlier today.

This is an interesting expectation given the latest manufacturing figures out of Beijing that showed a pickup in activity—for the first time in six months.

🍨

author: davooo
post:
$PSX Bullish on Energy

Commodity analysts at Standard Chartered have noted that energy markets kicked off the new year with an overly pessimistic view of oil demand, and sees an oil price rally unfolding in the coming months. StanChart estimates that January oil demand clocked in at 100.24 million barrels per day (mb/d), good for a 2.67 mb/d year-over-year increase and 0.25 mb/d higher than StanChart’s latest forecast. StanChart has now revised its earlier 2024 demand growth forecast to 1.69 mb/d from 1.64 mb/d previously. The analysts have also predicted a sustained period of inventory draws in H1-2024, with the cumulative draw coming in at 185 mb compared with a H1-2023 build of 230 mb. 

StanChart has predicted that global demand will hit a new all-time high of 103.01 mb/d in May, with June setting a new record at 103.62 mb/d while August demand is expected to be even higher at 104.31 mb/d. StanChart says tightening oil markets will continue to power the oil price rally!👅

author: davooo
post:
$PSX The AI boom and Big Tech might be hogging all the media limelight right now, but the smart money is quietly piling into energy stocks. Indeed, the energy sector is the most crowded of all 11 U.S. market sectors, with the sector’s favorite benchmark, Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE) up 10.7% in the year-to-date compared to a 7.9% return by the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLK) and 9.4% gain by the S&P 500.

But not everybody is bothered by the energy sector’s huge momentum. Morgan Stanley remains pessimistic about the U.S. stock market overall; however, MS has upgraded energy stocks to overweight from neutral, noting that energy companies have lagged the performance of oil, and the sector is favorably valued.

“Taking the Fed’s recent messaging into account and assuming it is less concerned about inflation or looser financial conditions, commodity-oriented cyclicals and energy in particular could be due for a catch-up," they have said.

🏖️

author: Ro_Patel
post:
Although, US gasoline consumption, in terms of product supplied to gas stations, rose by +1.5% in FY3, to 376M gallons/day, this was down by -3.9% from 2019 & back where it had first been 20 years ago, in 2003 at a 390M gallons/day. 
 
Gasoline consumption is determined by miles driven, which eked out a record in 2023 to 3,264B miles, however, per-capita gasoline consumption has plunged by -15% from 2003 & by -21% from 1978 despite US population growth. 
 
What gives?? 
 
Average fuel economy increased by +42% over the past 20 years from 14.6mpg in 1975 to 30mpg despite popularity of SUVs & trucks. 
 
Maintaining/increasing CAFE standards and recent popularity of hybrids & EVs will continue to apply pressure to the US gasoline industry. With less demand for gasoline domestically, the US has become a significant exporter of refined products - 21% of the record 10.1Mbpd of crude oil & petroleum products exports in FY23 were refined products.

Refinery Recovery Enhancing Demand

The resurgence of U.S. refineries is boosting demand for crude oil. Such geopolitical risks are typically bullish for crude oil prices due to the potential for supply disruptions.

author: davooo
post:
$PSX and SDPI

From OilPrice: “There’s just a lot of oil being left in the ground. Fracking’s been around for a really long time, but the science of fracking is not well understood,” Exxon Chief Executive Officer Darren Woods has said. Woods has revealed that Exxon is trying to improve productivity and lower costs by fracking more precisely along the well so that more oil-soaked rock gets drained and also by keeping the fracked cracks open longer so as to boost oil flows.

Luckily for the U.S. shale patch, there's already a proven technology that allows oil producers to give existing oil wells a second, high-pressure blast to increase output for a fraction of the cost of finishing a new well: shale well refracturing. Refracturing is designed to restimulate a well after an initial period of production, and can restore well productivity to near original or even higher rates of production as well as extend the productive life of a well. 

😊

Permian oil production to hit fresh record in 2024, Plains All American Pipeline reports

Lucia Kassai, Bloomberg February 09, 2024

(Bloomberg) – Oil production from the prolific Permian basin of West Texas and New Mexico is poised to rise to fresh record this year as drilling gets more efficient, according to a major U.S. pipeline operator.

The growth will happen due to drilling efficiencies even as the number of rigs remains stable, Chief Financial Officer Al Swanson said.

Three hundred rigs are “probably doing the work of close to 330 rigs 12 months ago,” he said. In the long term, Swanson also sees the industry increasing the amount of oil pumped from each well compared with the total volume available in the field. 

Last year, total U.S. oil production soared by 1 MMbpd as operators drilled deeper and longer wells — some stretching for miles — to extract more crude. The surge helped American crude exports rise to a record draw.

🤞

author: davooo
post:
$PSX and SDPI

Standard Chartered, JP Morgan Talk Oil Demand

The widespread perception among oil market participants that oil demand has been disappointing is not supported by data.

That’s the view of Standard Chartered analysts, according to a report sent to Rigzone this week, in which the analysts said 2024 oil demand growth forecasts from the “key assessment agencies have trended higher in recent months"

“Total November U.S. oil demand was revised higher by 910,000 barrels per day to 20.71 million barrels per day, taking the year on year increase to 2.45 percent from the initial estimate of a 2.05 percent decline,” they added.

“We estimate that the year on year increase in November was a strong 2.41 million barrels per day. Further, year on year growth of 2.30 million barrels per day in Q4-2023 exceeded the 1.92 million barrel per day year on year growth for 2023 as whole, suggesting that a widely discussed Q4 demand slowdown may have been an illusion"🤯

author: Ro_Patel
post:
RBOB Gas Cracks improved from last month as WTI falling faster than gasoline prices but still down significantly from summer driving season.  
 
US gasoline inventories rose by +5.4M barrels last week vs est of +1M barrels. Large inventory build came as refiners, returning from seasonal maintenance, ramped up rates by 0.7ppt to 90.5% of total capacity, but demand fell short of expectations - Product supplied of motor gasoline (demand), rose +3% last week to 8.46Mbpd. Demand during Thanksgiving week usually rivals summer demand (Weekly gasoline demand avg'd ~9Mbpd from April to Sept this year) 
 
US crude oil inventories are -1% below 5-year avg for this time of year 
 
US Gasoline inventories are -1% below 5-year avg for this time of year 
 
US Distillate inventories are -13% below 5-year avg for this time of year 
 
Will we see a ramp up in prices ahead of summer 2024 driving season or will increase in distillates create more gasoline by-product? 
 
$VLO $PSX $MPC $USO $UGA

$XOM $PSX $CVX $BP $MPC U.S. regulators say Gulf oil spill to impact several producers, Reuters says 
 
U.S. environmental regulators said earlier, that seven energy companies have been impacted by an oil spill close to the Main Pass Oil Gathering systems in the Gulf of Mexico, "that is estimated to have released more than a million gallons of crude oil," reports Reuters The almost 70 mile long pipeline was shuttered by the Main Pass Oil Gathering systems on Thursday morning "after crude oil was spotted around 19 miles offshore of the Mississippi River delta, near Plaquemines Parish, southeast of New Orleans," noted the story...Publicly traded companies in the space include BP (BP), Chevron (CVX), ConocoPhillips (COP), Exxon Mobil (XOM), Shell (SHEL) TotalEnergies (TTE), Marathon Petroleum (MPC), Phillips 66 (PSX), Tesoro (TSO), Valero (VLO) and Western Refining (WNR).

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$PSX 2 of 2 - Phillips 66 provides update on strategic priorities 
 
....to shareholders. The Board of Directors of Phillips 66 approved an additional $5 billion in share repurchase authorization. This is in addition to its previous authorization, which had approximately $3.1 billion remaining as of September 30. Since 2012, the Board has authorized share repurchases totaling $25 billion. Phillips 66's business transformation will deliver over $1 billion in run-rate cost and capital reductions by the end of 2023. The company is now targeting a $1.4 billion run-rate by the end of 2024. The company also plans to monetize non-core assets that are expected to generate over $3 billion in proceeds that will be deployed to further strategic priorities, including returns to shareholders. In August, Phillips 66 sold its 25% interest in South Texas Gateway Terminal for $275 million. The company's total proceeds from asset dispositions were $370 million through the third quarter of 2023."

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$PSX 1 of 2 - Phillips 66 provides update on strategic priorities 
 
The company said, "Phillips 66 is on track to exceed its original strategic priority targets. The company is successfully executing operational enhancements in Refining and delivering business transformation cost reductions. In Midstream, the implementation of the company's NGL wellhead-to-market strategy has exceeded expectations and enabled an increase to the synergy target. Given the company's substantial progress on these strategic priorities, combined with plans to increase commercial contributions, Phillips 66 is increasing its mid-cycle adjusted EBITDA growth target from $3 billion to $4 billion by 2025. Phillips 66 returned $6.7 billion through share repurchases and dividends since July 2022 and is on pace to exceed the original $10 billion to $12 billion target. The company is now increasing this target to a range of $13 billion to $15 billion and plans to return at least 50% of operating cash flow....

author: Ro_Patel
post:
OPIS: “I see it (gasoline prices) continuing to drop. Even if crude goes back to $100, it’s gonna be very difficult for gas to rally in the next 125 days” 
 
That’s b/c gas prices are being driven more by changes in the economics of oil refining than by changes in the broader oil industry 
 
Analyst thinks gas prices could fall to somewhere b/n $3.25- $3.50 & doesn’t expect trend to reverse until middle of 1Q24 
 
OPEC+ has cut production to boost oil prices - most is heavier grades that yields more diesel. US, meanwhile, producing more oil but light grades that yield less diesel 
 
Deisel margins jumped to $40/barrel, up from more “normal” margins of $10-$15 incentivizing refiners to make more diesel 
 
Refiners can’t make only diesel - each barrel of crude turns into a suite of refined products. Therefore, in order to make more diesel, you need to make more gasoline. But gasoline demand at 22-yr low. Gasoline cracks now below $10!! 
 
$VLO $UGA $MPC $PSX $CRAK

author: Ro_Patel
post:
Petroleum prices have risen in recent months, however, gasoline expenditures as a percentage of disposable personal income remain only slightly above the average since 2015 and below the average from periods when oil prices were last over $100 per barrel. 
 
Gasoline expenditures averaged 2.6% of disposable personal income in 1Q22 vs 2.4% average over the March 2015 to March 2020. But the rise in disposable personal incomes for the average American since 2008 has more than offset changes in gasoline prices. 
 
RBOB ran up during initial Russia invasion but has been range bound since b/n $2.40-$2.75

author: Ro_Patel
post:
US Crack remain elevated - lower crude price will increase margins for refiners. 
 
S&P Global: Margins will remain robust for at least the next year. Tightness in the diesel market is expected to ease in the 1H24 as new refining capacity comes online, while gasoline markets are dependent on the startup of Nigeria's Dangote refinery. 
 
1 key factor in the strength of US refined products are lower-than-average inventories of gasoline & diesel 
 
Virtually every US refiner said on their 2Q23 earnings call that they expect the tight supply/demand scenario for both fuels to continue into FY24. US distillate stocks remain -16% below the 5-year avg, w/ US Atlantic Coast diesel inventory -36% below 
 
UBS: "Given the low inventory levels & product shortages, we believe it's safe to assume there is meaningful upside to Q4 2023 ests" 
 
1% of the light-weight autos on American roads are electric & less if included all vehicles like commercial trucks.

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$VLO $PBF $PSX $MPC $DINO BofA downgrades all Buy-rated refiners to Neutral for 'strategic pause' 
 
BofA analyst Doug Leggate believes a "strategic pause is appropriate to re-assess next steps in what is a rapidly evolving landscape for refining." Accordingly, the firm downgraded all its Buy-rated refiners to Neutral. After 70% average share upside since BofA's "Regional Golden Age thesis" in March 2022, much of the reset in value has been discounted, the analyst tells investors in a research note. As such, the firm believes the stocks are no longer compelling and it sees risks that near-term margins have peaked. BofA downgraded Valero (VLO), PBF Energy (PBF), Phillips 66 (PSX), Marathon Petroleum (MPC) and HF Sinclair (DINO) to Neutral from Buy, leaving it with no Buy ratings across the U.S. refiners for now. The firm kept a Neutral rating Delek US (DK).

author: Ro_Patel
post:
$XLE $VLO $PSX $SU $MPC 
 
Money managers in the week to Aug. 1 boosted their net long holdings of RBOB futures to the highest since late Feb 2022 (highest since Russia invaded Ukraine). On a seasonal basis, this position is at a 3-year high. 
 
This year's seasonal rally has been super-charged by a 10-week low in supplies caused in part by refinery outages in the US east & Gulf Coasts and strong exports to global markets from the US, which typically imports from other countries to supplement its own production. 
 
Additionally, of concern, record Atlantic Ocean temps (highest in 120,000 years) could draw hurricanes into the Gulf of Mexico (nearly half of US oil refining capacity is located) & disrupts refineries (could damage oil refinery units, or cause refiners to temporarily shut them down until the storm passes) 
 
Gasoline futures have risen +14% this year vs +2% rise for US crude futures. Fuel strength pushed gasoline margins to one-year highs around $40 a barrel in July .

post:
Crude Complex Tearsheet: 
 
US refinery utilization decreased -0.8% w/w to 92.7%; last week’s move pulls the current rate to +1.7% above the s/adj'd 5-year avg. Last week’s decrease in run rates coincided w/ global gasoline refining margins increasing $3.85/bbl w/w & global distillate margins increasing $6.68/bbl w/w. 
 
Crude inventories ex. SPR drew -17Mbbl last week, beating analyst ests looking for a -0.8Mbbl draw & beating the API’s weekly data release pointing to a -15.4Mbbl draw. 
 
Net imports decreased -391Mbpd w/w, while total US refinery crude oil throughput decreased -138Mbpd & est'd US crude production was flat at 12.2Mbpd. 
 
Gasoline days of supply: 24.8 (-0.8 below the s/adj'd 5-year avg of 25.6 days) 
 
Jet Kerosene days of supply: 23.7 (-3.9 below the s/adj'd 5-year avg of 27.6 days)

In the US, jet fuel demand is approaching the 2019 level while TSA total traveler count already surpassed the previous record set in 2019. 
 
In summary, we continue to expect global jet fuel consumption will spike more than 1Mbpd this year of which China alone will account for 300-400Kbpd of the growth.

author: Ro_Patel
post:
$VLO $PSX $SU 
 
Tudor, Pickering, Holt: “It seems like every year someone announces a new refinery. But they almost never get built, given the challenges on economics & permitting" 
 
Last major US oil refinery was built in 1977 
 
6 US refineries w/ cumulative capacity to refine 750K barrels of oil closed during 2020 & 2021. $XOM recently added 250K barrel capacity. 
 
The high cost of opening a new refinery shows how inexpensive refinery stocks trade in terms of replacement value.  
 
For example, refiner $PBF has a market value of only about $5B, despite operating refineries with total capacity of more than 1Mbpd along w/ a new renewable diesel plant, & several other assets. If a new 250Kbpd refinery costs $5.6B to build, PBF should arguably be worth more.

author: Ro_Patel
post:
EIA: Petroleum product crack spreads often exhibit seasonality. During the summer months, the underlying commodity of the RBOB front month contract is required to be gasoline with a lower Reid vapor pressure (RVP) specification, a more expensive type of gasoline to refine. Starting March 1, RBOB futures prices tend to increase several cents from Feb averages to reflect the higher valued commodity. This, in turn, is reflected in a higher crack spread. The crack spread continues to remain fairly high through the late spring and into the summer months as the U.S. driving season picks up, resulting in higher domestic demand for the product. On Sept 1, the underlying commodity of the RBOB front month contract reverts back to a higher RVP specification, representing a lower-cost gasoline. The gasoline crack spread declines to reflect the lower price of gasoline. 
 
$XLE $VLO $PSX $MPC $UGA

author: Ro_Patel
post:
US gasoline prices rose for the 3rd week in a row, rising $.076 per gallon from a week ago to $3.65 per gallon yesterday reaching the highest level since November 2022. 
 
GasBuddy: "While the rising price of oil is likely the largest factor in rising gas prices, seasonal impacts continue to also exert pressure on gasoline prices. With the Northeast making the final step in the transition to summer gasoline this week, states in that region should expect a sharp rise in gasoline prices over the next week or two. Every other region has already seen the final step in the transition occur, so while other areas will see prices continue to slowly rise, the Northeast is likely to see a pretty hefty jump of 15-40 cents per gallon soon. Oil prices remain a wildcard, but we’re likely a few weeks away from seeing the national average peak. Whether it hits $4 per gallon or not is still perhaps a 50/50 chance.”

author: Ro_Patel
post:
$VLO $PSX $SU $MPC $IMO 
 
Typically, from February to May or June the US gasoline crack spread (the difference between the wholesale price of gasoline and the price of Brent crude oil) increases because of the shift to the more expensive, summer-grade gasoline & rising gasoline demand leading up to the summer. 
 
Over the past 5 years, the increase in the gasoline crack spread from February to June averaged almost 30 cents per gallon. Also because of the seasonal increase in gasoline demand from February to June, gasoline inventories have fallen by 13M barrels on average over the past 5 years.

author: Ro_Patel
post:
Most of the reserves in the world are sour. Sanctions now against Iran, Venezuela, & Russia are creating some tightness in that market for medium & heavy sour crude. Today, the US have sanctioned countries that represent about 1/3 of the total crude oil reserves worldwide. 
 
Typically in the market, gasoline & diesel sell for a premium compared to bottom of the barrel products such as fuel & asphalt which sells at a discount to crude oil. Therefore, lighter crude grades w/ a higher API gravity tend to yield more of the premium products such as gasoline & diesel, less fuel oil as the crude gets heavier, the API gravity drops the yield of gasoline & diesel drops.  
 
Additionally, inherent yields differ depending on the oil type feedstock of Light Sweet (WTI, LLS, Brent), Medium (Mars, Arab Medium) or Heavy Sour (Maya, WCS). 
 
The lighter crudes inherently yield more valuable products than heavy crudes. 
 
$USO $MPC $VLO $SU $PSX

Phillips 66 Announces Increase in Quarterly Dividend 
  
The board of directors of Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.05 per share on Phillips 66 common stock, representing an 8% increase. The dividend is payable on March 1, 2023, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on Feb. 21, 2023. “Rewarding shareholders through a secure, competitive and growing dividend is a priority for Phillips 66,” said Mark Lashier, President and CEO of Phillips 66. “We have increased the dividend 12 times since our inception in 2012, resulting in a 17% compound annual growth rate. author: mcalindenresearch
post:
US refinery runs have remained suppressed after a winter storm knocked millions of barrels of capacity offline along the US gulf coast last month. Further unexpected disruptions have occurred in the weeks since, coinciding with an ongoing uptick in planned outages for delayed rounds of maintenance. Refiner margins have jumped back to multi-month highs in the wake of output cuts, as prices of gasoline and diesel remain resurgent. 
 
Distillate inventories have been draining quickly in recent weeks and could get even tighter over the next month, as an EU embargo of Russian fuels is set to initiate on February 5. Though Exxon Mobil just completed a significant expansion of a major Texas refinery, large capacity additions remain increasingly rare due to aggressive anti-emission policies. $CRAK, $XLE, $VLO, $PSX, $MPC  
 
Read More: https://www.mcalindenresearchpartners.com/2023/01/25/refinery-margins-resilient-as-fuel-prices-rebound-disruptions-continue-to-roil-output/?utm_source=stocktwit&utm_medium=mrp_post&utm_campaign=stocktwit_tracking

post: $VET $MRO $PSX $XOM The CEO of Vermilion Energy only received CA$1.5m in total compensation for the year ending . That's clearly well below average, so at a glance, that arrangement seems generous to shareholders, and points to a modest remuneration culture. While the level of CEO compensation isn't a huge factor in my view of the company, modest remuneration is a positive, because it suggests that the board keeps shareholder interests in mind. It can also be a sign of a culture of integrity, in a broader sense. It appears their are much more buyers that feel the same. I am equally enamored by the ginormous buy that came between 26 and 27.  My gut says:we are unnoticed and  All aboard this train is about to leave you chasing!
